content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration          Enforcement          Executive Legal          Compliance
(916) 322.5660                                322.5601             322.6441             322.6444

August 12, 1977                                                    77-08-059

Robert L. Leslie
496 Alpine Lane
Sonora, California  95370

Dear Mr. Leslie:

     This is in response to your letter of 8 August 1977,
asking a variety of questions concerning the possibility
of the existence of a conflict of interest if you were to
engage in certain activities as a Sonora City Councilman.

     Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103 set forth the
general prohibitions against conflict of interest under the
Political Reform Act.1/ They provide, in relevant part:

     87100.  No public official at any level of
state or local government shall make, partici-
pate in making or in any way attempt to
use his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or
has reason to know he has a financial inter-
est.

     87103.  An official has a financial inter-
est in a decision within the meaning of
Section 87100 if it is reasonably foresee-
able that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its
effect on the public generally, on:

     ....(b)  Any real property in which the
public official has a direct or indirect
interest worth more than one thousand
dollars ($1,000);...


1/ The City Council of the City of Sonora has also adopted a
   Conflict of Interest Code containing similar provisions.
   As a City Councilman, you are also subject to the provisions
   of that Code.

Mr. Robert L. Leslie
August 12, 1977
Page Two

As we discussed previously, and agreed, it is clear that
your ability to continue and conclude construction of your
new home will have "a material financial effect, distinguish-
able from its effect on the public generally, on"...real
property in which you have an interest. See, Section 87103,
supra. Thus, as a Councilman, you may not generally "make,
participate in making or otherwise use your official position
to influence" decisions that will directly result in deciding
whether continued building may take place.

This prohibition does not, however, totally preclude you
from representing your own interests in this matter. The
Commission has interpreted the phrases "participating in the
making of a decision" and using (your) official position to
influence" by Regulation. 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(d)
specifically excludes from that concept:

Appearances by a public official as a
member of the general public before
an agency in the course of its pre-
scribed governmental function to repre-
sent himself or herself on matters related
solely to his or her personal interest...

Thus to the extent that you wish to represent your own
interests in this matter as a private citizen (and assuming
that you engage in no avenues of activity not open to other
members of the public and are bound by the same constraints
of action as other members of the public), such activity is
not prohibited by Government Code Section 87100.

I trust that partially answers several of your questions.
There remains, however, the question of what actions you may
legally take in your official capacity on behalf of third
party applicants (or complainants) with respect to matters
pending before the Building Department.2/ The answer is that
you are only prohibited from acting where it is "reasonably
foreseeable" that such actions will predictably result in the
approval of continued construction on your property. Based
on the facts as presented, and as we discussed, it does not
appear that it is reasonably foreseeable that the result will
occur. Stated simply, from what you have told me, it does
not appear that efforts on your part to resolve impasses
between third parties and the Building Department will fore-
seeably result in approval of your project. To the extent

_________________________________
2/ Assume for purposes of this discussion that none of the
third-party persons involved constitutes a "source of
income" to you within the meaning of Section 87103(c),
nor do you have any other financial interests in such
applicants.

Mt. Robert L. Leslie
August 12, 1977
Page Three

that they would, you should not participate in your official
capacity. Therefore, should different circumstances arise
in the future which make it more likely that approval of your
project would result from resolution of a third party dispute,
you should reassess the situation to determine if the criteria
governing disqualification are met.

I hope this helps resolve your situation. Thank you for
consulting us. If I can be of further assistance in this or
any other matter, please feel free to call me at (916)322-6444.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Kenneth W. Goshorn
Research Specialist III
Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:mfa

496 Alpine Lane
Sonora, Ca., 95370

August 8, 1977

Mr. Kenneth W. Goshorn
Conflict of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street Bldg.
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca., 95814

Dear Mr. Goshorn:

This is a letter to confirm in writing the things which we talked
about last week.

First, I am a Sonora City Councilman and am at the present time
building a new home. The City of Sonora Building Inspector has placed
a stop work order on my property because he feels that I am not
meeting the building code. I feel that I have met the building code,
but am still being stopped by the City Inspector. I do have a
contractor's license but have never to this date used it to build
for another person. I have had this license for the purpose of self
education and for the purpose of building rental buildings for myself
only in the past. I have built one building only (a rental fourplex)
since becoming elected to the City Council. Since the Building
Inspector has stopped construction of my new home, I have been concerned about due state laws pertaining to conflict of interest, and
need to know what rights I do or do not have during this period of
disagreement.

Given the above conditions, which are mine at present, could you
answer the following questions:

1. If I have complaints on the Building Department (a one
man operation) from the public at large do I have the right
to investigate as a Councilman on behalf of the public to
see if there is a problem? In answer to this question it
should be stated that I have no personal financial interest
in the owner, builder, or any other part of the particular
item, being questioned.

2. With the same conditions as question # 1 can I go through
city permits and files to check on the particular complaint
as a Councilman?

3. Can I inform the public or other Councilmen of any
irregularities which might be connected to questions # 1 and
# 2?

-D-

4. Can I call in any other government agencies to assist
   if I find irregularities under the terms of questions
   # 1 and # 2?
5. If any contractors or owners wish to protest the Building
   Inspector's performance do I have a right to participate with
   them as a Councilman if I have no financial interest in them
   or their projects?
6. Do I have the right to participate in any evaluations
   of this Building Department as a whole as a councilman?
7. Acting as a private citizen (not a Councilman) do I have
   the right to go through building permits and files to determine
   if I am being treated the same on construction of my own
   home(with a personal financial interest) as other private
   citizens have been treated on other similar construction
   situations.
8. If I find irregularities under the conditions described
   in item # 7, do I have the right as a private citizen (not
   a Councilman) to inform the public?
9. Can I appeal to any other government agencies to assist
   if I do find irregularities under the terms of question # 7?
10. Same as question # 5 but as a private citizen and not as
    a Councilman.

Even though you verbally discussed most of these items with me last
Friday, I think it best to have your opinions in writing should I
need them for future reference. Your prompt reply will be very much
appreciated.

                                                  Sincerely,

                                                  Robert L Leslie
                                                  Robert L. Leslie
